title: Fathoms
description: Dredge the depths, seize the seas, and find secrets untold deep below the waves...
body:
![Fathoms Title Art](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/69766990136744dac890240395c7cdf6f77b7017.png)

Fathoms is a datapack focused on a complex expansion of the seas of Minecraft, bringing a vast array of new mechanics, unique collectables, and macabre themes. Inspired by [Dredge](https://www.dredge.game/) as well as eldritch horror classics, it conjures an air of mystery leaving you wanting to dredge the depths for more.

# Contents
### Features
- 70+ fish found in a variety of biomes and conditions
- A complete overhaul of the fishing loot table, including a new bait system
- 2 new mini-biomes packed with exclusive mechanics and fish
- 2 interactive blocks to expand, diversify, and automate fishing
- A dredging system for diagetic metal farming and discovering ancient secrets
- A cryptic ritual system, empowering you on and off the water
- A vast collection of trinkets and novelty items found at sea
- _Tons of secrets_
### Extras
- A massive new advancement tab featuring intuitive explanations
- Full Peaceful mode support
- Custom statistics menu (1.21.6+)
- Subtle, slightly pretentious, hopefully intriguing worldbuilding

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/tI0eEG16w_A" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

# Note on the Wiki
As a creative decision, Fathoms' wiki will not be maintained by developers. It may contain some information provided by the community, but it is not a 100% reliable source. If you are having trouble with doing something as the wiki instructs, assume it is outdated. **All of Fathoms' content has been precisely designed to be discovered ingame via *advancements*, the *recipe book*, and *diegetic clues*. Additonally, Fathoms is best enjoyed blind...**

# Frequently Asked Questions
## Major Questions

**Q:** Why is the pack is not appearing on my Modrinth installation?
**A:** Modrinth does not properly install datapacks into worlds directly. You will need to manually insert the datapack and resourcepack into your world's datapack folder and your resourcepack folder respectively.

**Q:** Why is this visual thing not looking right?
**A:** Install the resourcepack. If you are having trouble finding the resourcepack on Modrinth, click the version you are downloading, *not* the download button. Scroll down and download the resourcepack.

**Q:** Is Fathoms compatible with <x>?
**A:** Fathoms is not designed to be compatible with any fishing mods or datapacks. It may be compatible with some datapacks that make minor changes to fishing loot tables (like [Myriad](https://modrinth.com/datapack/myriad)), which can be done by [Welding](https://weld.smithed.dev/) packs.

## Content Questions
**Q:** Is this a datapack?
**A:** Yes. We all make datapacks exclusively, not mods. They all use modern technology (and Smithed conventions!) to ensure the experience is integrated just as a mod.

**Q:** When are you updating this pack to this Minecraft version?
**A:** We don't know, don't bother us about it.

**Q:** Will there be a Bedrock port for Fathoms?
**A:** We do not make content for Bedrock.

**Q:** Can you/I port to this Minecraft version/can you/I port to Bedrock?
**A:** No.
## Ingame questions

**Q:** How do I spawn in items/mobs?
**A**: For items, run `/loot give @s loot <datapack>:item/<item>`. For mobs, run `/function <datapack>:commands/summon/<mob>`.

**Q:** I am having an issue that has not been fixed by installing the resourcepack!
**A:** Datapacks are not compatible with lots of server software such as PaperMC made by lazy devs who do not take them into account. If you are running a non-vanilla server software, this may be the reason. Fabric, Quilt, Forge, and Neoforge are *probably* compatible.

**Q:** Why am I not catching any of the custom fish?
**A:** Fathoms is not compatible with other mods and datapacks that modify fishing loot tables. We can not fix this.
